ir_specs.statement: The `-A` option select the base in which file offsets are printed.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: Select the base in which file offsets are printed.

ir_specs.statement: The `--endian=` option reorder input bytes, to handle inputs with differing byte orders, or to provide consistent output independent of the endian convention of the current system.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: Reorder input bytes, to handle inputs with differing byte orders, or to provide consistent output independent of the endian convention of the current system.

ir_specs.statement: The `-j` option skip bytes input bytes before formatting and writing.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: Skip bytes input bytes before formatting and writing.

ir_specs.statement: The `-N` option outputs at most bytes bytes of the input.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: Output at most bytes bytes of the input.

ir_specs.statement: The `-S` option instead of the normal output, output only string constants : at least bytes consecutive printable characters, followed by a zero byte (ASCII NUL).
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: Instead of the normal output, output only string constants : at least bytes consecutive printable characters, followed by a zero byte (ASCII NUL).

ir_specs.statement: The `-t` option select the format in which to output the file data.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: Select the format in which to output the file data.

ir_specs.statement: The `-v` option outputs consecutive lines that are identical.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: Output consecutive lines that are identical.

ir_specs.statement: The `-w` option dump n input bytes per output line.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: Dump n input bytes per output line.

ir_specs.statement: The `-a` option outputs as named characters.
The `-c` option outputs as printable single byte characters, C backslash escapes or 3 digit octal sequences.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: Output as printable single byte characters, C backslash escapes or 3 digit octal sequences.

ir_specs.statement: The `-d` option outputs as unsigned decimal two-byte units.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: Output as unsigned decimal two-byte units.

ir_specs.statement: The `-l` option outputs as decimal long ints.
The `-o` option outputs as octal two-byte units.
The `-s` option outputs as decimal two-byte units.
The `-x` option outputs as hexadecimal two-byte units.
